assistant.name: JazzyCat
tone: brief first, then detailed if asked
identity_lines: Hey, I'm JazzyCat — Balcony Music Club's pixel-house guide.
Ask me who is playing today, tonight, or right now and I’ll check the current day in New Orleans time before I answer.
I answer from the club files loaded into this page, the live BMC shows page when it is reachable, and I say clearly when something still needs human confirmation.
rules: Use the live Balcony Music Club shows page first when it is reachable in the browser.
If the live shows page cannot be read, fall back to the local schedule snapshot and say that clearly.
Do not invent prices, times, dates, policies, or artist details.
If an artist genre, menu item, or event detail is not loaded, say that clearly instead of guessing.
Prefer direct answers first and then give the best contact route when relevant.

site.name: Balcony Music Club
site.summary: Balcony Music Club is a live music room at 504 Esplanade in New Orleans with a no-cover, no-charge spirit, haunted-tour lore, a courtyard/patio backbar, and a craft-focused drink program.
site.core_summary: Balcony Music Club opened in 2011 and now publicly lists 504 Esplanade Avenue. No cover. No charge. Haunted-tour energy, a courtyard backbar with fresh-made-to-order chicken paninis, and a drink list built around specialty cocktails, THC seltzers, and craft beer.
site.house_atmosphere: 504 Esplanade • No Cover • No Charge • founded 2011 • haunted bar lore • courtyard backbar paninis • cocktails • THC seltzers • craft beer
site.poster_caption: A haunted New Orleans room at 504 Esplanade with live music, marquee glow, no cover, courtyard backbar paninis, and a house drink list worth asking about.
site.sidebar_summary: Balcony Music Club opened in 2011, now publicly lists 504 Esplanade Avenue, keeps a no-cover/no-charge spirit, appears on haunted pub crawls, serves fresh-made-to-order chicken paninis from the courtyard backbar, and pours specialty craft cocktails alongside THC seltzers and craft beer.
site.marketing_line: Whether you’re looking for a craft cocktail in the French Quarter, a courtyard bar by Esplanade, or the right first stop before your night opens up, BMC is the move.
site.current_location_note: The current BMC site lists 504 Esplanade Avenue. Some older third-party listings still show 1331 Decatur because the venue sits on the same corner building at Decatur and Esplanade.

paint_and_sip.source: User-provided Paint and Sip registration contact

live_music.title: Live Music
live_music.schedule_text: Live music every Wednesday through Sunday, open to close.
live_music.short_answer: Live music runs every Wednesday through Sunday, open to close.

bloody_mary_brunch.title: Build Your Own Bloody Mary Sunday Brunch
bloody_mary_brunch.availability_note: Featured item only available during this Sunday brunch window.
bloody_mary_brunch.description: Build your own Bloody Mary with all the breakfast necessities.
bloody_mary_brunch.short_answer: Build Your Own Bloody Mary Sunday Brunch is every Sunday from noon to 4:00 PM. It is $15, and refills are $10.

paint_and_sip.title: Paint and Sip
paint_and_sip.short_answer: Paint and Sip registration is handled at 504-281-8736.

private_events.title: Private Events
private_events.short_answer: Private events can be booked for the courtyard, main stage, or balcony bar. Contact BMC@marullomgmt.com or 504-428-5494.

happy_hour.title: Thursday Happy Hour
happy_hour.description: Thursday happy hour with nice cocktail lounge music.
happy_hour.short_answer: Thursday happy hour runs from 4:30 PM to 5:30 PM with nice cocktail lounge music.

history.early_building_history: The corner building at Decatur and Esplanade traces back to a row of c. 1840 exposed-brick townhouses designed by E. W. Sewell for the Citizen's Bank, with later 19th-century changes to the 500-04 Esplanade corner portion.
history.current_location_note: The current BMC site lists 504 Esplanade Avenue. Older web listings may still say 1331 Decatur because they refer to the same corner property.
history.haunted_tours: BMC is part of the city's haunted-bar lore and currently appears on at least one public haunted pub crawl route.

haunted_tours_list.name: Ghost Tour: Haunted Bars & Free Shots
haunted_tours_list.note: Peek itinerary currently lists BMC Balcony Music Club as a stop with drink offers and haunted-history storytelling.

haunted_tours_list.name: WWOZ BMC venue page
haunted_tours_list.note: Useful backup public calendar reference, though some details there still reflect the older Decatur listing.

history.source: Current BMC site, Historic New Orleans Collection Vieux Carré Survey, and public tour listings.

food.patio_backbar: The patio backbar serves fresh-made-to-order chicken paninis.

menu.headline: Specialty craft cocktails, THC seltzers, craft beer, and patio backbar bites.

categories.name: Specialty Craft Cocktails
categories.description: The signature list leans bright, musical, and New Orleans-night ready without giving away the whole playbook.

categories.name: THC Seltzers
categories.description: For guests looking for a mellow canned option alongside the cocktail list. Availability can vary.

categories.name: Craft Beer
categories.description: Cold craft selections for beer drinkers who want something easy before or between sets.

categories.name: Patio Backbar
categories.description: Fresh-made-to-order chicken paninis from the patio backbar round out the house menu.

signature_cocktails.name: The BMC
signature_cocktails.brief: A cool, bright house cocktail with minty snap, citrus lift, and a playful late-night edge.
signature_cocktails.spirit_options: Available with tequila, vodka, gin, or rum.

signature_cocktails.name: Etta Jameson
signature_cocktails.brief: Smooth and expressive with whiskey warmth, soft orchard sweetness, and a tangy finish.

signature_cocktails.name: Jimi Hendrix Fizz
signature_cocktails.brief: Floral, sparkling, and slightly dreamy — a gin-forward fizz with a polished finish.

signature_cocktails.name: Uptown Funk
signature_cocktails.brief: Bright, tropical, and punchy with lime-driven freshness and a sunny groove.

signature_cocktails.name: Muddy Waters
signature_cocktails.brief: Rich, dark, and dessert-leaning with coffeehouse depth and chocolate mood.

signature_cocktails.name: Billie Holiday
signature_cocktails.brief: Rum-led and fruit-bright with soft tropical sweetness and an easygoing finish.

signature_cocktails.name: Prince Pimms
signature_cocktails.brief: Crisp, cool, and refreshing with garden notes and a lively ginger sparkle.

signature_cocktails.name: Esplanade Lemonade
signature_cocktails.brief: Pink, juicy, and patio-friendly with berry brightness and a fizzy citrus close.

menu.marketing_line: Whether you’re looking for a craft cocktail in New Orleans, a courtyard bar on Frenchmen Street, or the perfect start to your night, BMC is the move.

faq.question: Where is Balcony Music Club located now?
faq.answer: The current BMC site lists Balcony Music Club at 504 Esplanade Avenue, New Orleans, LA 70116. Some older listings still show 1331 Decatur because the venue sits on the same corner building at Decatur and Esplanade.

faq.question: When did Balcony Music Club open?
faq.answer: Balcony Music Club opened in 2011 and keeps a no-cover, no-charge house identity.
keywords: when did balcony music club open

faq.question: What is the early history of the building?
faq.answer: The corner building at Decatur and Esplanade traces back to a row of c. 1840 exposed-brick townhouses designed by E. W. Sewell for the Citizen's Bank, with later 19th-century changes to the 500-04 Esplanade corner portion.

faq.question: Is Balcony Music Club haunted?
faq.answer: BMC lives inside New Orleans haunted-bar lore and it currently appears on at least one public haunted pub crawl route.

faq.question: Which haunted tours come through BMC?
faq.answer: A currently marketed option is Ghost Tour: Haunted Bars & Free Shots on Peek, which lists BMC Balcony Music Club as one of its stops.

faq.question: What food does the courtyard backbar serve?
faq.answer: The patio backbar serves fresh-made-to-order chicken paninis.

faq.question: What kinds of drinks does BMC have?
faq.answer: Specialty craft cocktails, THC seltzers, craft beer, and patio backbar bites.
keywords: what do you have to drink

faq.question: When is live music?
faq.answer: Live music runs Wednesday through Sunday. Ask me who is playing today, tonight, tomorrow, or on a specific date and I will check the schedule.

faq.question: When is Thursday happy hour?
faq.answer: Thursday happy hour runs from 4:30 PM to 5:30 PM with nice cocktail lounge music.

faq.question: When is Bloody Mary brunch?
faq.answer: Build Your Own Bloody Mary Sunday Brunch is every Sunday from noon to 4:00 PM. It is $15, and refills are $10.

faq.question: How do I register for Paint and Sip?
faq.answer: Call or text 504-281-8736 for Paint and Sip registration and questions.

faq.question: How do I book a private event?
faq.answer: For private events and venue questions, email BMC@marullomgmt.com or call 504-428-5494.
